free_text: The emission of photoneutrons from natural lithium.
In  this experiment two detection systems were
used.  The first system had a  detection  efficiency
independent  of  the  initial  energy of the emitted
photoneutrons.   The   second    system    responded
essentially  to  neutrons  having energies below 0.6
MeV.
Lithium target,  99.9% purity, had a form of circular
discs  3.5 cm diameter.  The target was 14.6 cm long
and had a net mass of 70 g.
The absolute yield from the natural copper 17 MeV was
used for normalization of lithium yield.
Its' value   at   17   MeV  was  found  to  be:  (0.9
This method  responded essentially to neutrons having
energies below 0.6 MeV.
The photoneutron  yield  curve of natural lithium has
been determined for incident bremsstrahlung energies
up  to  18.5  MeV.  This  yield curve shows no gross
structure in the region of 17 MeV  as  indicated  in
some  previous  measurements.  the  resulting  cross
section indicates the presence of three high  energy
levels in Li-7 at 13.6, 15.3 and 16.5 MeV.
The     method     of
smoothing of experimental data was described.
Corrected by V.Varlamov, V.Mclane.

Data must be multiplied by 1.0e+07.
Statistical errors are presented.  The  total
background yield for bremsstrahlung energies between
7.0 MeV and 18.5 mev was measured.  At  10  MeV  the
background  amounted  to  3%  of  the  total lithium
yield, at 17 MeV - 6%.

The cross sections were calculated from the yield
curve by using the method of inverted matrix developed
by Penfold and Leiss with 1 MeV step.
